intro: Phaseoloides championii, also known as Himalayan kidney bean, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family, native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Phaseoloides championii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine systems.
